steps.title: Spot the contrast in distances
steps.content: The passage first establishes JWST's distance from Earth (one million miles), then introduces Hubble's distance (340 miles). The transition must signal we're comparing these two positions.

steps.title: Match the comparison signal
steps.content: In comparison works because it's the only transition that sets one thing against another for contrast. The other three all signal the wrong kind of relationship—sequence, addition, or causation—rather than comparison. That matches choice C — C is correct.
